title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined

input_row.title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined
input_row.description: Use network policies to isolate traffic in the cluster network.
input_row.rationale: Running different applications on the same Kubernetes cluster creates a risk of one compromised application attacking a neighboring application. Network segmentation is important to ensure that containers can communicate only with those they are supposed to. A network policy is a specification of how selections of pods are allowed to communicate with each other and other network endpoints. Network Policies are namespace scoped. When a network policy is introduced to a given namespace, all traffic not allowed by the policy is denied. However, if there are no network policies in a namespace all traffic will be allowed into and out of the pods in that namespace.
input_row.audit: Run the below command and review the NetworkPolicy objects created in the cluster. kubectl get networkpolicy --all-namespaces ensure that each namespace defined in the cluster has at least one Network Policy.
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation and create NetworkPolicy objects as needed. See: https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/network- policy#creating_a_network_policy for more information.
input_row.impact: Once network policies are in use within a given namespace, traffic not explicitly allowed by a network policy will be denied. As such it is important to ensure that, when introducing network policies, legitimate traffic is not blocked.
input_row.default_value: By default, network policies are not created. Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be fully verified via GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI.
- The audit field provides a specific command (`kubectl get networkpolicy --all-namespaces`) which can be executed to obtain the required information.
- Network policies are a standard Kubernetes service feature which can be queried from the Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) context.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_namespace_network_policy_defined

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to configure and access the GKE cluster.
- Execute the command `kubectl get networkpolicy --all-namespaces` to retrieve all network policies across all namespaces in the cluster.
- Enumerate the namespaces and check that each namespace has at least one defined Network Policy.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: Each namespace is associated with at least one Network Policy.
  - Fail: At least one namespace is found without a Network Policy.

**Reference GCP API calls:**
- API: Kubernetes Engine API for interacting with Pods and Cluster (for managing network policies).
- The specific Kubernetes API to list network policies (`NetworkPolicies`).

**Example of Pass/Fail criteria validation:**
- If the output of `kubectl get networkpolicy --all-namespaces` returns at least one policy under each namespace, then that namespace complies with the control. Otherwise, report it as non-compliant.
